title: Small Coffee Table (Super Simple Collection)
summary: A coffee table on two 2x4 H-frame ends — legs and supports joined into a simple ladder shape, topped with a two-board 1x10 shelf and a 1x12 top.
description:
Two 2x4 H-frames — legs joined by top and bottom supports — carry the whole table, the same repeating-frame idea as this catalog's Super Simple console but at coffee-table height and with just two end frames instead of three. Build both frames identically before joining anything else.

The 1x10 boards rest across the frames as a lower shelf, and the 1x12 boards cap the top — both simple flat assemblies with no aprons or face frame, screwed directly into the frame supports. **No pocket holes or hidden joinery here** — every connection is a straightforward screw, which is the entire point of the "Super Simple" approach.

Sand and finish before or after final assembly, whichever is easier given the tools on hand — this build is forgiving either way.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the two H-frames
steps.body: Assemble two identical H-shaped end frames from the legs and leg supports.

steps.title: Attach the shelf
steps.body: Attach the two shelf boards across the lower supports of both frames.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the two top boards across the upper supports of both frames.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Small coffee table on two simple 2x4 H-frames
